models.generation_context: 1st-gen Venue (QX), introduced for US MY2020; minor 2024 refresh; MY2026 drops Limited trim (top equipment rolled into SEL), adds two-tone roof option to SEL
models.model_summary: Subcompact crossover and Hyundai's lowest-priced model in the US. Front-wheel-drive only, powered by a 121-hp 1.6L I4 with CVT. For 2026 the Limited trim is dropped and its equipment (wireless phone charging, heated front seats, heated mirrors, two-tone roof options) is rolled into the new SEL.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Venue-specific VDS score. Hyundai brand placed in the top tier of the 2025 JD Power Initial Quality Study but the current MY VDS measures 3-year-old cars and has not been issued. Consumer Reports has not yet released 2026 predicted reliability for Venue.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Venue-specific APEAL score. Hyundai brand placed near the top of the 2025 IQS but Venue is not separately measured in the 2025 APEAL study.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the Venue the cheapest new car in America and a strong value buy: praise focuses on a comprehensive standard SmartSense safety suite, easy-to-use 8-inch touchscreen with wired Apple CarPlay/Android Auto, and Hyundai's 5yr/60k bumper-to-bumper warranty. Recurring criticisms are sluggish acceleration with the 121-hp 1.6L, soft seats, and abundant road and engine noise at highway speed.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews for the 2026 Venue are still light given recent-MY release. Common praise from prior-MY owners centers on fuel economy, low purchase price, and standard tech; common complaints are highway acceleration, ride quality on rough pavement, and limited rear-seat space.

Venue did not earn a 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick (only six TSP+ and one TSP went to Hyundai for 2026, none to Venue) and NHTSA has not posted a 2026 overall rating. 0-60 not published by Hyundai (third-party estimates typically 9-10 sec).

models.generation_context: 7th-gen Elantra (CN7), introduced for MY2021; mid-cycle refresh applied for MY2024; MY2026 continues refresh with new alloy wheel designs and earned the 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick award
models.model_summary: Compact front-wheel-drive sedan offered with three powertrain choices: 147-hp 2.0L NA I4 (CVT), 139-hp 1.6L hybrid (6-speed DCT), and 201-hp 1.6L turbo (7-speed DCT) marketed as N Line. The Elantra N performance variant is treated here as a separate model. For 2026 Hyundai earned the IIHS Top Safety Pick for Elantra and offers Hybrid in Blue, SEL Sport, and Limited trims.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Elantra-specific VDS score. Hyundai brand finished No. 3 overall in the 2025 JD Power Initial Quality Study (mass-market segment). Consumer Reports has rated prior-MY Elantras (2023-2025) above average for reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not separately published a 2026 Elantra APEAL score. Brand-level scores place Hyundai near the top of mass-market brands.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2026 Elantra for its sharp exterior design, generous standard tech (dual 10.25" displays on SEL Sport+), strong fuel economy (especially the Hybrid Blue at 54 combined MPG), and class-leading 5yr/60k bumper-to-bumper warranty. The 2.0L base engine is described as adequate but unexciting; the 1.6T N Line adds welcome punch. Recurring complaints concern road and tire noise on N Line wheels and a firmer ride on Sport-suspended trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Recent-MY owner reviews are thin for 2026 specifically. Prior-MY owners (2024-2025) cite fuel economy, comfortable highway ride, and standard tech as the most common praise; common complaints involve CVT droning under hard throttle and a small-feeling trunk lid opening.

models.generation_context: Performance variant of 7th-gen Elantra (CN7); introduced for MY2022; MY2026 continues with minor visual refresh; TCR Edition announced for limited production
models.model_summary: Hyundai's hot compact-sedan performance flagship. Sole-trim model with a 276-hp 2.0L turbo I4 (10-hp overboost to 286 hp via N Grin Shift for 10 seconds), standard six-speed manual or optional eight-speed N wet DCT, electronically-controlled limited-slip differential, and N-tuned chassis. 0-60 mph in roughly 5.0 seconds (DCT) per Hyundai-published timings.

reliability.summary: JD Power and Consumer Reports do not separately publish reliability data for Elantra N. As a performance-focused sub-line of a generally reliable Elantra platform, expect Hyundai brand-level reliability with caveats around DCT wear and brake/tire consumption typical of track-capable cars.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not separately rated Elantra N for APEAL. Brand-level satisfaction is high.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Elantra N as the segment value champion against the VW GTI and Honda Civic Si, praising the snarling 2.0T, electronically-controlled LSD, eager 8-speed wet DCT with launch control and N Grin Shift overboost, and standard performance hardware (Pirelli P Zero tires, 14.2-inch front brakes). Trade-offs are a stiff ride on broken pavement, modest interior materials versus more-expensive rivals, and 23 combined MPG.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are limited for 2026 but prior-MY ownership feedback praises engagement, value, and the unique sound of the 2.0T with N Sound+ system; common complaints involve highway ride harshness and tire wear from the standard summer Pirellis.

powertrain.transmission: 6-speed manual (standard); 8-speed N wet dual-clutch automatic with paddle shifters optional

models.generation_context: 8th-gen Sonata (DN8), introduced MY2020; significant mid-cycle refresh applied MY2024; MY2026 adds new Hybrid Blue trim (54 combined MPG) under $30k and earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+
models.model_summary: Midsize sedan offered with three powertrain choices: 191-hp 2.5L NA I4 (8-speed automatic) on SE and SEL Sport, 290-hp 2.5T (8-speed wet DCT) on N Line, and 192-hp 1.6L hybrid (6-speed DCT) on Hybrid Blue/SEL/Limited. For 2026 Hyundai adds Hybrid Blue (a value-oriented hybrid at $29,050 base) and earned IIHS TSP+ for Sonata.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Sonata-specific VDS. Hyundai brand finished No. 3 overall in the 2025 JD Power IQS. Consumer Reports rated recent-MY Sonatas (2023-2025) as average to above-average for reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Sonata-specific APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the refreshed Sonata for its assertive curved-display dashboard with two 12.3-inch screens, comfortable highway ride, strong hybrid efficiency (51 combined MPG on Blue), and competitive pricing in a shrinking midsize-sedan segment. The N Line's 290-hp 2.5T offers genuine speed but isn't as engaging as smaller dedicated sport sedans. Common critiques are dated infotainment software ergonomics and front passenger seat that doesn't power-adjust on lower trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are still light for 2026 specifically. Prior-MY owners cite roomy interior, strong tech, and good fuel economy as the most-mentioned positives; common complaints are touch-sensitive dashboard controls (introduced with 2024 refresh) and road noise at highway speeds.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen Kona (SX2), introduced for MY2024; MY2026 simplifies trim lineup — drops former SEL, SEL Convenience, N Line, N Line S trims and adds SEL Sport and SEL Premium; earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+
models.model_summary: Subcompact crossover offered in four ICE trims. Base SE and SEL Sport use a 147-hp 2.0L NA I4 with IVT (CVT); SEL Premium and Limited step up to a 190-hp 1.6L turbo I4 with 8-speed automatic. AWD is a $1,500 option on most trims. The Kona Electric is excluded for MY2026 (skipping the model year in US — returning as a 2027 model).

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Kona-specific VDS score. The 2nd-gen Kona was redesigned for MY2024, so 3-year-old data is not yet available. Hyundai brand finished No. 3 overall in 2025 JD Power IQS.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not separately published a 2026 Kona APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2nd-gen Kona's roomier rear seat and cargo area versus the prior gen, distinctive futuristic exterior design, generous standard tech (dual 12.3-inch displays standard on every trim), and strong safety credentials (2026 IIHS TSP+). The 2.0L NA engine on SE/SEL Sport is described as sluggish, especially with AWD; the 1.6T on SEL Premium/Limited is markedly better. Recurring critiques: the redesigned interior feels less premium than the smaller Venue's design and the touch-sensitive HVAC controls.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are still light for 2026. Prior-MY 2nd-gen owners cite versatile cargo space, standard dual 12.3-inch screens, and Hyundai's warranty as strongest praise; common complaints are acceleration on the base 2.0L engine and road noise.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Tucson (NX4), introduced for MY2022; mid-cycle refresh applied for MY2025 (redesigned front fascia, updated rear lighting); MY2026 carries refresh and earns IIHS Top Safety Pick+
models.model_summary: Compact 2-row crossover offered with three powertrain choices: 187-hp 2.5L NA I4 (8-speed automatic) on SE/SEL/XRT/SEL Premium/Limited; 231-hp 1.6T hybrid (6-speed automatic) on Blue SE/SEL/SEL Convenience/Limited Hybrid; and 268-hp 1.6T plug-in hybrid (13.8-kWh battery, 33-mile EV range) on SEL PHEV/Limited PHEV. XRT trim adds adventure-oriented styling.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Tucson-specific VDS. Consumer Reports rated 2024-2025 Tucson as 'much better than average' for predicted reliability, with the hybrid scoring particularly well.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Tucson-specific APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Tucson's bold exterior styling, spacious rear seat, and roomy 38.7 cu ft cargo area (74.8 with seats folded); the standard 12.3-inch dual displays; and the genuinely efficient Hybrid (38 combined MPG). The PHEV offers 33 miles of EV range and 268 hp combined output. Common critiques are the 2.5L NA engine's modest acceleration on the heavier Limited AWD trim, and Hyundai's revised touch-capacitive HVAC controls drew mixed reviews after the 2025 refresh.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are still light for 2026. Prior-MY (2024-2025) Tucson owners cite cabin space, fuel economy of the Hybrid, and aggressive design as top positives; common complaints involve highway wind noise and the 8-speed automatic's hesitation at low speed.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Santa Cruz (NX4); introduced for MY2022; refreshed MY2025 with new fascia and interior tech; MY2026 carry-over with adventure-oriented XRT trim and Limited as top
models.model_summary: Compact sport-adventure pickup based on the Tucson platform. Two powertrains: 191-hp 2.5L NA I4 (FWD standard, AWD optional) on SE/SEL/SEL Activity; 281-hp 2.5T (standard AWD with dual-clutch transmission) on XRT and Limited. 4-foot bed with 27 cu ft cargo, up to 5,000 lb towing on Turbo trims, up to 1,720 lb payload.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Santa Cruz-specific VDS. Consumer Reports rates Santa Cruz as average for predicted reliability; some prior-MY 2.5T transmission concerns have been reported but no widespread service campaigns.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not separately published a 2026 Santa Cruz APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Santa Cruz as a uniquely sized urban pickup blending SUV comfort with light-duty utility. Praise focuses on the 281-hp turbo engine on Limited/XRT (5,000 lb towing), refined ride versus body-on-frame midsize trucks, and standard dual 12.3-inch displays. Common critiques: small bed (4-foot length, 27 cu ft); the 2.5L NA base is underpowered when loaded; cabin storage solutions feel utility-focused but plastics are commodity-grade.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are still limited for 2026. Prior-MY owners praise the unique form factor, urban maneuverability, and bed flexibility with the optional power tailgate; common complaints are road noise on the all-terrain-style XRT tires and modest cargo capability vs full-size trucks.

models.model_summary: Midsize 3-row SUV redesigned for 2024 with upright, rugged styling. Two powertrains: 277-hp 2.5T (8-speed automatic) on SE/SEL/XRT/Limited/Calligraphy; 231-hp 1.6T hybrid (6-speed automatic) on Hybrid SE/SEL/Limited/Calligraphy. professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the redesigned Santa Fe's boxy, retro-rugged styling, expanded usable cargo area (40.5 cu ft behind 2nd row), standard 12.3-inch dual displays, and Limited's new captain's chair 2nd row for 2026. Powertrain critique improves for 2026 with the switch from the prior dual-clutch to a conventional 8-speed automatic. Common concerns: 21-inch wheels on Calligraphy ride harshly, and the Hybrid's 36 combined MPG trails the Toyota Grand Highlander Hybrid Max.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews for 2026 are limited (recent release). Prior-MY owners praise rear-seat space, cargo-area flexibility, exterior design, and the standard tech load; common complaints are the prior dual-clutch transmission behavior (fixed for 2026) and forward visibility from the upright A-pillar.

models.generation_context: All-new 3rd-gen Palisade (LX3) for MY2026; longer 199.2 in (200.4 in for XRT Pro), wider stance, more angular design with multi-blade grille; introduces Hyundai's first 3-row hybrid SUV powertrain and first XRT Pro off-road trim; earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+
models.model_summary: Three-row midsize SUV redesigned for 2026 with bolder styling, more cabin space, and a brand-new hybrid powertrain. Two powertrains: 287-hp 3.5L V6 (8-speed automatic) and 329-hp 2.5T hybrid (6-speed automatic). Seven trims including new XRT Pro off-road variant with 8.4-inch ground clearance and Terrain Mode. Seating up to 8 passengers (standard bench) or 7 with optional captain's chairs.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet rated the all-new 2026 Palisade (3rd-gen). Consumer Reports does not have predicted reliability data for the redesigned vehicle. Hyundai brand reliability remains in the top tier of mass-market brands per the 2025 JD Power IQS.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Palisade APEAL score (all-new model). The 2nd-gen Palisade ranked highly in APEAL through 2024-2025.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the redesigned Palisade one of the best three-row SUVs in the segment: praise centers on cabin space and material quality (especially Calligraphy's Nappa leather and suede headliner), the new Hybrid's 329 hp output and ~34 MPG combined, and the rugged XRT Pro's genuine off-road equipment. The bold angular face polarizes opinions. Critics note that the Calligraphy approaches $60k, putting it against Genesis GV80 and other premium 3-row SUVs.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are limited (all-new for 2026). Early ownership impressions praise interior space, build quality, and the optional hybrid efficiency; common complaints focus on the polarizing exterior styling and significant price climb on Calligraphy and XRT Pro.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Ioniq 5 (NE1), introduced for MY2022 on Hyundai's E-GMP 800V platform; MY2026 brings native NACS (Tesla) port, refreshed front lighting, and significant $7,600-$9,800 price cuts across the lineup; XRT off-road variant new for MY2025
models.model_summary: Electric crossover and Hyundai's halo EV. Available with 63 kWh battery (SE Standard Range, 245-mi range) or 84 kWh battery (SE/SEL/XRT/Limited; 245-318 mi range depending on configuration). RWD trims use 225-hp rear motor; AWD trims combine front + rear motors for 320 hp combined. For 2026 gains native NACS port and significant price reductions of $7,600-$9,800. The high-performance Ioniq 5 N is treated as a separate model.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Ioniq 5-specific VDS score. Consumer Reports has rated 2023-2025 Ioniq 5 as average for reliability with some early software/infotainment issues largely resolved by 2024.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 Ioniq 5-specific APEAL score. Prior years scored highly in EV-specific surveys for design and tech.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rank the Ioniq 5 as one of the best mainstream EVs: praise focuses on retro-futurist styling, 800V architecture enabling 10-80% DC fast charging in ~18 minutes (350 kW peak), spacious flat-floor interior, and standard dual 12.3-inch displays. The native NACS port new for 2026 expands charging compatibility. Common critiques: rear vision is limited by thick rear pillar, and combined range under 320 miles trails segment leaders like Tesla Model Y Long Range.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are still limited for 2026. Prior-MY owners cite charging speed (800V), unique exterior design, and Tesla Supercharger access (via NACS adapter) as top positives; common complaints are heat pump noise in cold weather and infotainment slow-loading.

models.generation_context: Performance variant of 1st-gen Ioniq 5 (NE1); introduced for MY2025 in US; MY2026 carry-over with native NACS port and minor visual refresh
models.model_summary: Hyundai's halo EV performance model. Sole-trim with 601-hp dual-motor AWD (641 hp with N Grin Boost for 10 seconds), 84-kWh battery (~221-mile range), and track-focused chassis. 0-62 mph in 3.4 seconds, 160 mph top speed. Features N e-Shift simulated 8-speed paddle-shift, N Active Sound+, N Drift Optimizer, and dedicated front cooling/aero. Built on the same E-GMP platform with 800V architecture as standard Ioniq 5.

reliability.summary: JD Power and Consumer Reports have not separately rated Ioniq 5 N for reliability. As a high-performance EV variant of a generally reliable platform, expect Hyundai brand-level reliability with potential track-related wear on tires and brakes.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not separately rated Ioniq 5 N for APEAL.

professional_reviews.summary: The Ioniq 5 N is unanimously hailed as the most engaging high-performance EV available, redefining what's possible for an electric performance car. Praise focuses on the simulated 8-speed N e-Shift with paddle shifters, N Drift Optimizer, dual-motor 641 hp output with launch control, and N Sound+ audio that adds character. Critiques: 221-mile EPA range is short for a $68k EV, and the 21-inch high-performance tires are loud and wear quickly on track.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are limited (low-volume performance halo). Early adopters cite the N e-Shift feel, sound, and dual-motor torque as transformative; common complaints are real-world track range (under 100 mi at limit) and tire/brake consumption.

models.generation_context: Performance variant of 1st-gen Ioniq 6; new for MY2026 in US; standard Ioniq 6 (non-N) is discontinued for MY2026 in the US (Hyundai only offers the N model in the US for 2026 per Oct 2025 reports)
models.model_summary: Hyundai's high-performance EV sedan, all-new for MY2026 in the US. Sole-trim with 601-hp dual-motor AWD (641 hp with N Grin Boost for 10 seconds), 84-kWh battery (~210-220 mi est. range), and track-focused chassis derived from the Ioniq 5 N. 0-62 mph in 3.2 seconds, 160 mph top speed. Native NACS charging port. The standard (non-N) Ioniq 6 is discontinued for MY2026 in the US.

reliability.summary: All-new MY26 performance EV with no track record. JD Power VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles, so no model-specific 2026 score exists for the Ioniq 6 N. Brand-level Hyundai placed above the 204 PP100 industry average in the 2026 JD Power VDS (industry coverage indicates Hyundai-Kia are in the upper tier of mass-market brands; Kia: 193 PP100). Consumer Reports has not published a predicted-reliability score for the Ioniq 6 N. Expect typical performance-EV consumables wear; the Ioniq 5 N twin has been generally well-reviewed for early reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the Ioniq 6 N (low US volume).

professional_reviews.summary: Early-coverage reviewers describe the Ioniq 6 N as a more aerodynamic sedan counterpart to the Ioniq 5 N — same 601-hp dual-motor powertrain with N Grin Boost overboost to 641 hp, N e-Shift simulated 8-speed, and N Drift Optimizer, in a more streamlined body. The lower drag improves both acceleration and range slightly compared to the Ioniq 5 N. Critiques echo the 5 N: short EPA range under heavy use, premium $68k pricing.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are not available as the Ioniq 6 N is brand new in the US market for 2026 with first deliveries expected mid-to-late 2026.

models.generation_context: All-new 1st-gen Ioniq 9 (ME); Hyundai's flagship 3-row electric SUV, built at the brand's new Metaplant America factory near Savannah, Georgia; first US deliveries Q1 2026; 800V E-GMP platform shared with Ioniq 5/6 with stretched 122.3-inch wheelbase
models.model_summary: Hyundai's flagship three-row electric SUV. Built on the 800V E-GMP platform with a 110-kWh battery delivering up to 335 miles of EPA-rated range (RWD trim). Three powertrain configurations: 215-hp single rear motor (S trim, RWD), 303-hp dual-motor AWD (SE/SEL), and 422-hp Performance dual-motor AWD (Limited/Calligraphy variants). Six trims and seating for 7 (bench) or 6 (captain's chairs). Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+.

reliability.summary: All-new flagship 3-row EV with no track record. JD Power VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles, so no model-specific 2026 score exists for the Ioniq 9. Brand-level Hyundai placed above the 204 PP100 industry average in the 2026 JD Power VDS (Kia: 193 PP100; Hyundai-Kia in upper tier of mass-market). Consumer Reports has not yet published a predicted-reliability score. Built at Hyundai's new Metaplant America (HMGMA) facility; early-production reliability is unknown.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score exists for the all-new Ioniq 9.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the Ioniq 9 one of the best three-row EVs on sale, citing its 335-mile RWD range, spacious cabin with optional captain's chairs and a sliding center console, and Hyundai's signature 800V architecture enabling 350 kW DC fast charging in ~24 minutes. The 422-hp Performance variants are quick (~5.2 sec 0-60). Critiques: relatively high starting price (S trim approaches $60k loaded with destination), polarizing pixel-themed exterior, and no native Tesla Supercharger NACS port on launch units (adapter required).

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are limited (Ioniq 9 deliveries began Q1 2026). Early-adopter impressions praise interior space, charge speed, and 2nd-row captain's chair configurations with the Sliding Universal Island 2.0 console.

models.generation_context: All-new 2nd-gen Nexo for MY2026; Hyundai's hydrogen fuel-cell SUV; sold exclusively in California through dedicated hydrogen-infrastructure dealers; major upgrade to the 1st-gen Nexo (2019-2024) with more powerful 201-hp motor, 80 kWh buffer battery, and 7.8-second 0-100 km/h time
models.model_summary: Hyundai's hydrogen fuel-cell electric SUV (FCEV), all-new for 2026 and sold exclusively in California due to hydrogen-infrastructure constraints. Single 201-hp electric motor powered by a 110 kW fuel cell stack and 80 kWh buffer battery. EPA-rated 442-mile range on Nexo Blue (most efficient) or 395 miles on standard Nexo. 5-minute hydrogen refuel time. Two trims: base and Limited (estimated upper trim).

reliability.summary: All-new 2nd-generation hydrogen fuel-cell SUV; no track record. JD Power VDS does not score the Nexo at model level given very low US volume (California-only sales). Brand-level Hyundai placed above the 204 PP100 industry average in the 2026 JD Power VDS. Consumer Reports has not published a predicted-reliability score. Prior-gen Nexo (2019-2024) had limited owner data due to sales scale.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). The Nexo has not historically received standalone APEAL scoring given very low US volume.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the all-new 2026 Nexo as a major upgrade over the prior generation: praise focuses on the 201-hp motor (vs 161 hp prior), 5-minute hydrogen refuel time, 442-mile range on the Blue trim, and improved 7.8-second 0-100 km/h acceleration. Most reviewers emphasize that the Nexo's relevance is heavily constrained by California-only hydrogen fueling infrastructure (hundreds of stations, mostly metro LA/Bay Area).

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are not yet available for the all-new 2026 Nexo (production deliveries beginning 2026). Prior-gen owner concerns centered on hydrogen-station availability and pump downtime.
